what: The opt-in documentary-Koine levers measured on the pinned PapyGreek test fold: one sequential run per shipped variant, after all iteration on the document-disjoint dev folds.
protocol: greek.use_neural_pipeline(); the lever toggles; greek.evaluate_on_papygreek(); CPU sequential (canonical for this fold); fold papygreek-fold-v2 (sha 75304be2...). The pinned baseline row is unchanged; each variant is an opt-in registry sub-row.

levers.A_coordinator_reconciliation: conservative: relabels only the closed coordinator set (Smyth 2163 ff.) when the model emitted the always-wrong X/b reading; UPOS -> CCONJ, XPOS pos-code -> c; the aggressive d/ADV fold-in is measured-against (literary dev -5.24 UPOS) and not shipped as default
levers.B_lemma_oov_rescue: fires only on the neural identity fall-through (lemma_resolved False); consults seed then the guarded paradigm table (use_paradigms); the ending-rule tier deliberately excluded (dev-measured break-even documentary, net-negative literary); rescues carry SEED/PARADIGM evidence classes, never NEURAL

anatomy_consistency_check: the conservative UPOS lift (+3.26 points) equals the fold's 784 CCONJ->X errors / 24,105 words (3.25 points) to within rounding: the lever corrected essentially the whole always-wrong class and nothing else; UFeats/lemma/UAS/LAS/CLAS are byte-identical to the baseline run, as designed

dev_measurements_summary.note: all lever iteration happened here, never on the test fold; papygreek-dev-tagging 327 sents / 6,389 tokens; ud perseus dev 1,137 sents / 22,135 tokens (literary regression check)
